content: Which one of the following statements regarding a Rankine cycle is FALSE?

options.content: Superheating the steam in the boiler increases the cycle efficiency.
The pressure at the turbine outlet depends on the condenser temperature.
Cycle efficiency increases as condenser pressure decreases.
Cycle efficiency increases as boiler pressure decreases.

explanation:
The Rankine cycle is a thermodynamic cycle used in steam power plants. Among the given statements about the Rankine cycle, we need to identify the false one.

Option A: Superheating the steam in the boiler increases the cycle efficiency.

This is true because superheating adds more energy to the steam, increasing the work output of the turbine and hence improving cycle efficiency.

Option B: The pressure at the turbine outlet depends on the condenser temperature.

This is also true. The condenser's temperature determines the saturation pressure at which the steam condenses, which is directly related to the turbine outlet pressure.

Option C: Cycle efficiency increases as condenser pressure decreases.

This is true. Lowering the condenser pressure lowers the temperature at which heat is rejected, increasing the difference in temperature between the heat source and sink, which improves cycle efficiency according to the second law of thermodynamics.

Option D: Cycle efficiency increases as boiler pressure decreases.

This statement is false. In fact, increasing the boiler pressure generally increases the thermal efficiency of the Rankine cycle because it raises the average temperature at which heat is added to the cycle.

The correct answer is:

Option D: Cycle efficiency increases as boiler pressure decreases.

content: The INCORRECT statement about regeneration in vapor power cycle is that

options.content: It increases the irreversibility by adding the liquid with higher energy content to the steam generator
Heat is exchanged between the expanding fluid in the turbine and the compressed fluid before heat addition
The principle is similar to the principle of Stirling gas cycle
It is practically implemented by providing feed water heaters

The specific enthalpy and specific entropy of steam at the turbine inlet are $3344 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~kg}^{-1}$ and $6.5 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~kg}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}$, respectively. The turbine isentropic efficiency is 0.9 , and the mass flow rate of steam at the turbine inlet is $102 \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$. The turbine power output is ________ MW (rounded off to 1 decimal place). Properties of saturated liquid and saturated vapor at turbine exit pressure

$$
\text { Saturated liquid water }
$$		 $$
\text { Saturated liquid vapor }
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
&\text { Specific entropy }\\
&\left(\mathrm{kJ} \mathrm{~kg}^{-1}\right)
\end{aligned}
$$		 $$
\begin{aligned}
&\text { Specific entropy }\\
&\left(\mathrm{kJ} \mathrm{~kg}^{-1} / \mathrm{K}^{-1}\right)
\end{aligned}
$$
 $$
\begin{aligned}
&\text { Specific enthalpy }\\
&\left(\mathrm{kJ} \mathrm{~kg}^{-1}\right)
\end{aligned}
$$		 $$
\begin{aligned}
&\text { Specific entroopy }\\
&\left(\mathrm{kJ} \mathrm{~kg}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}\right)
\end{aligned}
$$
341 1.1 2645 7.6
explanation: $$ \begin{aligned} &\eta_T=0.9, \quad \dot{m}=102 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{sec}\\ &\begin{aligned} S_1 & =S_2 \\ 6.5 & =101+x(7.6-1.1) \\ x & =0.83076 \\ h_2 & =341+0.83076(2645-341) \\ & =2255.092 \\ W_T & =\left(h_1-h_2\right) \eta_T=(3344-2255.092) \times 9 \\ & =980.016 \mathrm{~kJ} / \mathrm{kg} \\ P & =\dot{m} \times W_T=102 \times 980.016 \\ & =99961.726 \mathrm{~K}=99.961 \mathrm{MW} \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

content: In a steam power plant based on Rankine cycle, steam is initially expanded in a high-pressure turbine. The steam is then reheated in a reheater and finally expanded in a low-pressure turbine. The expansion work in the high-pressure turbine is 400 kJ/kg and in the low-pressure turbine is 850 kJ/kg, whereas the pump work is 15 kJ/kg. If the cycle efficiency is 32%, the heat rejected in the condenser is ________ kJ/kg (round off to 2 decimal places).
explanation:
Given :

  • Work of high-pressure turbine, $ w_{\text{t, high}} = 400 \, \text{kJ/kg} $

  • Work of low-pressure turbine, $ w_{\text{t, low}} = 850 \, \text{kJ/kg} $

  • Pump work, $ w_p = 15 \, \text{kJ/kg} $

  • Cycle efficiency, $ \eta_{\text{Rankine}} = 32\% $

First, calculate the total work output of the turbines :

$ w_t = w_{\text{t, high}} + w_{\text{t, low}} = 400 + 850 = 1250 \, \text{kJ/kg} $

The net work output of the cycle is:

$ w_{\text{net}} = w_t - w_p = 1250 - 15 = 1235 \, \text{kJ/kg} $

Using the cycle efficiency equation:

$ \eta = \frac{w_{\text{net}}}{Q_{\text{add}}} $

Substitute the given efficiency :

$ 0.32 = \frac{1235}{Q_{\text{add}}} $

Solving for $ Q_{\text{add}} $ :

$ Q_{\text{add}} = \frac{1235}{0.32} = 3859.37 \, \text{kJ/kg} $

The efficiency can also be written in terms of heat rejected:

$ \eta = 1 - \frac{Q_{\text{rejected}}}{Q_{\text{added}}} $

Solving for $ Q_{\text{rejected}} $ :

$ 0.32 = 1 - \frac{Q_{\text{rejected}}}{Q_{\text{add}}} $

$ 0.68 \times 3859.37 = Q_{\text{rejected}} $

$ Q_{\text{rejected}} = 2624.375 \, \text{kJ/kg} $

Therefore, the heat rejected in the condenser is 2624.38 kJ/kg when rounded to two decimal places.

content: In the Rankine cycle for a system power plant the turbine entry and exit enthalpies are $$2803$$ $$kJ/kg$$ and $$1800$$ $$kJ/kg$$, respectively. Which combination of the following statements is correct?

The incorporation of re-heater in a steam power plant.

$$P:$$ always increases the thermal efficiency of the plant.
$$Q:$$ always increases the dryness function of steam at condenser inlet.
$$R:$$ always increases the mean temperature of heat addition
$$S:$$ always increases the specific work output.

Determine the correctness or otherwise of the following Assertion $$(a)$$ and the Reason$$(r).$$

Assertion(A): In a power plant working on a Rankine cycle, the regenerative feed water heating improves the efficiency of the steam turbine.

Reason(R): The regenerative feed water heating raises the average temperature of heat addition in the Rankine cycle.

options.content: Both $$A$$ and $$R$$ are true and $$R$$ is the correct Reason for $$A$$
Both $$A$$ and $$R$$ are true but $$R$$ is NOT the correct reason for $$A$$
